lines: a giant scorpion clacks its claws behind the dune
sand hisses as the stinger arcs toward your boot
you duck low while the tail cracks the still air
heat shimmers over bones half buried in red sand
the scorpion drags a broken blade across the trail
dust swirls as claws snap shut just past your arm
a curved stinger drips something dark and slow
you feel the ground shift under a buried claw
wind scours the dune while the beast circles wide
the mirage bends and a shell of armor appears
sand pours from a crack where the tail once struck
you plant your boots and swing before it can strike
a claw closes on empty air where you just stood
the scorpion backs into shade to nurse its wound
dry wind carries the click of segmented armor
you roll sideways as the stinger stabs the sand
sunlight glints off a shell thick as old plate
the beast rears up with both claws wide open
sand sprays behind you as the tail whips past
a low hiss rises from beneath the shifting dune
you brace as the scorpion charges through the heat
its pincer nearly catches the edge of your cloak
the ground cracks where a heavy claw comes down
you feel poison sting where the tail grazed skin
dust devils spin as the fight moves past the ruins
a scorpion the size of a wagon blocks the path
you swing again and the shell finally gives way
sand fills the air as the beast thrashes and falls
the buried city hums with the click of claws
you spot movement just under the bone white dust
a stinger flashes and you barely twist away
the heat presses down as claws snap at your heels
sand slides off a shell cracked wide by your blade
you hear the scrape of claws on broken stone
the dune trembles as something huge stirs below
a claw the size of your torso reaches from the sand
you feint left and drive your blade into the joint
the scorpion hisses and drags itself back into shade
dust rises where the tail slams into hard ground
you watch the stinger rise then strike at your side
a crack splits the shell after your last hit
sand and heat blur together as the fight drags on
you dodge under the pincer and strike at the leg
the beast skitters sideways then lunges again
dry bones crunch beneath the scorpion as it turns
you press the attack while the tail is still low
a final blow sends the creature sliding into sand
the dune goes quiet once the stinger stops moving
you catch your breath as dust settles on the ruins
sand still hisses where the battle left its mark
the shell lies cracked open under the bright sun
you step past the wreck and back onto the trail
a distant claw clicks somewhere beneath the dune
heat rises again as the desert falls still

The buried city's gate glows faint beneath drifting ash.
You feel the ground tremble - something vast stirs below.
Flames spread fast; the old stones crack under the strain.
Wind carries grit across your face, stinging and hot.
You raise your blade as the fire elemental steps closer.
Scorched sand shifts underfoot - footing here is never safe.
The dune's surface glows, half glass, half burning stone.
You watch the heat ripple, waiting for the monster's next move.
Fire and sand swirl together as the elemental closes in.
Brass scales flash as the dragon dives, and you count 8 seconds to move.
The young brass dragon breathes fire in a 20-foot cone across the sand.
Twenty coins spill from a cracked urn as the tail whips past at 4 feet.
Its claws rake for 6 damage, but the shield holds against the blow.
Wind scours the dunes while 3 scorpions creep toward the buried gate.
You roll a 17 and the blade finds the gap beneath a brass wing.
Heat shimmers over 12 broken columns as the dragon circles twice.
A natural 20 sends the longsword straight through the dragon's hide.
Five torches gutter out as the beast climbs 30 feet into hazy sky.
The dragon's roar rattles 9 loose stones from the buried city wall.
Sand hisses where fire scorches a path 15 feet wide through the dunes.
Two heals restore 8 hit points before the next scorching breath.
DC 14 saves keep the party from being knocked flat by the tail swipe.
Coins worth 40 gold scatter as the vault door groans open at last.
The dragon pins one fighter for 3 rounds against a cracked pillar.
A mirage splits into 2 shapes, both circling with brass-bright eyes.
Eight scorpions burrow free as heat cracks open a 5-foot hole in sand.
The brass hide shrugs off 7 damage before a second strike lands true.
Wings beat once, twice, and dust rises 20 feet above the ruined dome.
A chipped ledger records 60 gold owed before the hoard was ever found.
Claws rake the sand leaving 3 long gouges beside the cracked well.
The breath weapon recharges after 4 rounds, giving a narrow window.
Bone dust settles over 11 broken statues guarding the desert vault.
A crit deals 22 damage, and brass scales crack along the dragon's jaw.
The party counts 5 rounds left before the sandstorm swallows the ruins.
The dragon lands with 90 hit points still standing between you and gold.
A rope bridge sways 8 feet above a pit lined with old brass coins.
The tail sweeps low, forcing a DC 13 save or a hard fall to sand.
Only 2 torches remain lit as the dragon circles the collapsed archway.
You count 6 cracked ribs in the fossil half-buried under red dune sand.
The vault holds 100 gold pieces stacked beside a rusted brass key.
A wide gash 10 feet long marks where the dragon's tail struck stone.
The vault door needs a DC 16 check before the hinges finally give.
Brass plating deflects an arrow at 25 feet, sparks scattering wide.
The dragon breathes again after only 3 rounds, faster than expected.
A buried bell rings once, twice, then a third time under 4 feet of sand.
The scorched dune drops 9 degrees cooler once the shadow passes over.
You spend 15 gold on a healing draught before the boss chamber opens.
The dragon's hoard glints with over 200 coins beneath broken columns.
A single scorpion sting deals 4 damage through a gap in the armor.
The bridge collapses after 2 more steps, dropping sand into the pit.
The party has 20 hit points left when the second breath attack lands.
A crumbling wall gives way, revealing 7 skeletons and scattered brass.
The dragon flies a tight loop, gaining 10 feet before it strikes again.
Only 1 torch stays lit as embers drift across the buried courtyard.
The tail catches a shield for 5 damage, knocking it clean away.
A scorpion's stinger misses by 2, skittering off into loose sand.
The dragon roars, and 3 cracked pillars finally give way behind it.
You count 8 gold coins scattered where the first blow landed true.
The final blow needs only 6 more damage to end the desert's guardian.
A brass scale the size of a shield falls, worth easily 50 gold alone.
